steps.title: Step 2
steps.content: The confirmed linear factors are $x$, $(x+5)$, $(x-8)$, and $(x-3)$. Any product of two or more of these must also divide $f(x)$.

steps.title: Step 3
steps.content: Check each choice by factoring: A = $(x-5)(x+3)$ (roots $5, -3$ — not zeros of $f$); B has negative discriminant so no real roots; C = $x^2 - 11x - 24$ would need roots summing to 11 and multiplying to $-24$, but $8 \cdot 3 = 24$ not $-24$; D = $x(x-8)$ uses two confirmed zeros.

steps.title: Step 4
steps.content: $x^2 - 8x = x(x-8)$ must be a factor because both $x$ and $(x-8)$ are factors of $f(x)$.

choiceEliminations:
• Choice A — $x^{2}-2x-15$: Factors as $(x-5)(x+3)$; roots 5 and $-3$ are not zeros of $f$. ✗
  • Choice B — $x^{2}-3x+40$: Discriminant $= 9 - 160 = -151 \lt 0$; no real roots at all, let alone the given zeros. ✗
  • Choice C — $x^{2}-11x-24$: For roots from our zero list to multiply to $-24$ they would need opposite signs, but $8 \cdot (-3)$ is not a zero pair we have. ✗
  • Choice D — $x^{2}-8x$: Factors as $x(x-8)$; roots 0 and 8 are both confirmed zeros of $f$. ✓
D is correct.
